places.name: Flippin' Burgers
places.order: Double cheeseburger; fries; milkshake
places.famousFor: The spot that kicked off Stockholm's burger craze; sustainable locally-sourced ingredients in a retro diner setting
places.goodToKnow: Simple short menu (burgers, shakes, craft beer, natural wine); can get busy.

places.name: Bastard Burgers
places.order: A signature city-named burger; loaded fries; vegan options
places.famousFor: Streetfood-style burgers with Norrland roots and NYC inspiration; burgers named after world cities; strong vegan range
places.goodToKnow: Fast-casual chain; full vegan menu; late hours Fri/Sat. Multiple branches across Sweden (also Norway/Finland/US). · Location needs verification

places.name: Garage bar
places.order: G-Burger; Nashville Hot Chicken; The Luther (donut burger)
places.famousFor: Americana biker-themed grill that put Hoganas on Sweden's burger map; the G-Burger
places.goodToKnow: Booking advised; also does chicken & waffles and ribs. (Located in Hoganas, NW Skane - not Stockholm.) · Location needs verification

places.name: Funky Chicken Foodtruck
places.order: Triple Cheese Burger; weekly special; upgrade to double patty
places.famousFor: Iconic Stockholm food truck repeatedly voted Sweden's best burger; weekly-rotating menu
places.goodToKnow: Food truck, ~125 SEK per burger; menu changes weekly; check socials for location/hours before going.

places.name: Barrels
places.order: House special burger; bearnaise or cured-meat burger; craft beer
places.famousFor: Polished Stockholm burgers-and-beer concept with a rotating creative burger menu
places.goodToKnow: No advance booking; gluten-free buns and good veg/vegan options; multiple central branches.

places.name: Bun Meat Bun
places.order: Classic smash cheeseburger; fries; homemade milkshake
places.famousFor: Long-running Stockholm smashburger spot (since 2014); heavily-crusted patties on daily-baked potato buns
places.goodToKnow: Fresh chuck patties, locally-baked buns; several Stockholm locations.

places.name: Mancini
places.order: Mushroom risotto and mushroom ravioli; fillet of beef with salsa verde
places.famousFor: Ranked Sweden's best Italian restaurant by Gambero Rosso every year 2019-2025 (Three Forks)
places.goodToKnow: Mid-to-upscale; long-running classic Italian; reservations recommended, especially weekends. Tunnelgatan 1A, Norrmalm

places.name: Giro
places.near: Nearest metro Hotorget (~200m); next to Miss Clara hotel
places.order: Marinara; Margherita; seasonal pies (e.g. pumpkin puree, smoked mozzarella, zucchini)
places.famousFor: Authentic Neapolitan pizzeria by the Nobis Hospitality Group in collaboration with Naples' legendary Da Michele; Neapolitan pizzaioli and Italian-imported ingredients.
places.goodToKnow: Noted for Stockholm's best gluten-free pizza; ranked among the city's top restaurants.

places.name: 450 Gradi
places.order: Margherita; Neapolitan pizzas; homemade gelato
places.famousFor: Award-winning Neapolitan pizza baked at 450C by a Naples-born pizzaiolo
places.goodToKnow: On Lidingö island (not Nacka); co-owned by ex-footballer Jesper Blomqvist; among Europe's 50 best pizzerias

places.name: Sturehof
places.near: Stureplan; metro Östermalmstorg (Exit Stureplan), 2 min walk
places.order: Sole Walewska; butter-fried herring with mash; shellfish platter; skagen toast
places.notes: Svenska Brasserier flagship; World of Mouth best-seafood list
places.famousFor: Stockholm's grand seafood brasserie since 1897 — marble bars, Jonas Bohlin-designed rooms and one of the best fish counters in town, open every day until late
places.goodToKnow: Fish/seafood dominates so pork-free ordering is effortless (some charcuterie on menu; no halal cert, alcohol served). Kid-friendly for a Stureplan institution. Book 3-7 days ahead for dinner; walk-ins at the bar. $$$

places.name: Wedholms Fisk
places.near: Arsenalsgatan at Berzelii Park; metro Kungsträdgården 3 min
places.order: Poached turbot with horseradish and brown butter; grilled Dover sole; pike-perch with chanterelles
places.notes: Visit Stockholm + World of Mouth seafood institutions
places.famousFor: Stockholm's purest fish restaurant since 1985, founded by Bengt Wedholm and among the first in Sweden to hold a Michelin star — turbot and Dover sole treated with old-school reverence
places.goodToKnow: Almost entirely fish — ideal pork-free fine dining (alcohol served, no halal cert). Quiet, white-tablecloth room; better with the older kids or as a parents' meal. Book ~1 week ahead. $$$$

places.name: B.A.R.
places.near: Blasieholmsgatan behind the Grand Hôtel; metro Kungsträdgården 5 min
places.order: Whole grilled catch of the day from the counter; grilled langoustine; fish burger
places.notes: Thatsup + World of Mouth best fish restaurants
places.famousFor: Fish-market concept near the Grand Hôtel — walk to the ice counter, pick your whole fish, crab or cut and have it grilled; casual, loud and very Stockholm
places.goodToKnow: Choose-your-own fish makes pork-free easy and fun for kids (meat cuts also on ice — simply skip; alcohol served). Relaxed dress; book a few days ahead for weekend dinner. $$$

places.name: Lisa Elmqvist
places.near: Inside Östermalms Saluhall on Östermalmstorg; metro Östermalmstorg 1 min
places.order: Toast skagen; boiled lobster; fish soup; gravlax with dill-stewed potatoes
places.famousFor: Fourth-generation fishmonger inside Östermalms Saluhall since 1926 with a seven-metre fish counter — the definitive saluhall lunch, recommended by the Michelin guide
places.goodToKnow: Pure fish/shellfish — the easiest premium pork-free meal in town. No reservations at the counter seats; come before 12:00 or after 14:00 to avoid the queue. $$$

places.name: Tysta Mari
places.near: Inside Östermalms Saluhall; metro Östermalmstorg 1 min
places.order: Shrimp sandwich (räksmörgås); Swedish fish gratin; meatballs (ask — usually beef/pork mix, choose fish instead)
places.famousFor: Beloved saluhall bar-bistro with roots back to 1834 — comfort classics, pastries and people-watching at the marble counter of Stockholm's grandest food hall
places.goodToKnow: Plenty of fish/shrimp dishes for pork-free ordering; classic meat dishes may contain pork so ask. Walk-in counter culture, no booking needed. The hall also has a Beirut Café counter (halal Lebanese). $$

places.name: Rökeriet Fjäderholmarna
places.near: Boat from Strandvägen/Nybrokajen or Slussen to Fjäderholmarna (~25 min), then 3 min walk
places.order: House hot-smoked salmon; smoked shrimp; archipelago seafood platter; kids' fish & chips
places.famousFor: Working smokehouse on Stockholm's nearest archipelago island — fish smoked on site, eaten on a waterside deck; the easiest true skärgård experience from the city
places.goodToKnow: Smoked-fish menu = naturally pork-free; ice-cream kiosk for the kids. Summer is peak — book a table and check last boat times back. Perfect golden-hour dinner in August. $$$ · Location needs verification

places.name: Fjäderholmarnas Krog
places.near: Same 25-min boat from Strandvägen or Slussen; restaurant at the harbour
places.order: Fish and shellfish stew; fried Baltic herring; västerbotten cheese pie; seafood platters
places.famousFor: White-tablecloth archipelago dining right on the quay of Stora Fjäderholmen with sunset views over the water — a Stockholm summer classic
places.goodToKnow: Fish-led menu with meat and vegetarian options — pork easy to avoid. Summer-season only (open in August); book ahead for sunset tables, families welcome. $$$

places.name: Gondolen
places.near: Katarinahissen lift at Slussen; metro Slussen 2 min
places.order: Toast skagen; poached cod; classic beef Rydberg (pure beef); Swedish meatballs (ask mix)
places.notes: Falstaff 90-years feature + Vogue Scandinavia reopening piece
places.famousFor: Reopened 2023 by Svenska Brasserier (Sturehof, Riche) after full renovation — suspended 33 m above Slussen with the best dining-room view in Stockholm; 90 years of history
places.goodToKnow: Fish and beef classics make pork-free simple (some pork dishes on menu; alcohol served). The view wows kids and adults alike; window tables go first — book 1-2 weeks ahead in summer. $$$

places.name: Pelikan
places.near: Blekingegatan; metro Skanstull 5 min
places.order: Fried herring with mash and lingon; salmon dishes; veal liver; NOTE meatballs and the famous pork knuckle are pork-based — herring is the move
places.famousFor: Art Nouveau beer hall from 1904 with vaulted painted ceilings — the reference room for old-school Swedish home cooking on Södermalm
places.goodToKnow: Honest note: this is pork-and-schnapps heartland (signature pork shank, pork-mix meatballs) — but herring, salmon and veal are excellent pork-free orders. Loud, historic, kid-tolerant early evening. Book a few days ahead. $$

places.name: Tradition
places.near: Österlånggatan in Gamla Stan; metro Gamla Stan 5 min
places.order: Meatballs (ask for the mix — often beef/veal), fried herring, salmon pudding, raggmunk with lingonberries (skip the pork side)
places.famousFor: Small Old Town room dedicated to the Swedish home-cooking canon — pyttipanna, kåldolmar, raggmunk — done properly at fair prices steps from the Royal Palace
places.goodToKnow: Classic menu includes pork (raggmunk usually comes with fried pork — order it without); fish classics abound. Compact and popular with families sightseeing in Gamla Stan; book for dinner. $$

places.name: Kvarnen
places.near: Tjärhovsgatan; metro Medborgarplatsen 2 min
places.order: Fried herring; salmon with dill potatoes; beef Rydberg; meatballs (ask mix)
places.famousFor: Cathedral-like beer hall pouring since 1908, immortalised in Stieg Larsson's Millennium books — generous, well-priced Swedish classics in a working-class institution
places.goodToKnow: Go EARLY with the family (17-19) — it becomes a loud football/bar crowd later. Pork on menu but herring/salmon/beef options are solid; alcohol central to the room. No-fuss booking. $$

places.name: Prinsen
places.near: Mäster Samuelsgatan; metro Östermalmstorg 3 min
places.order: Biff Rydberg (the signature, pure beef); wallenbergare (veal); butter-fried herring; skagen
places.famousFor: Wood-panelled artists' brasserie cooking since 1897 — biff Rydberg and wallenbergare in a room of white cloths, brass and literary history
places.goodToKnow: Beef/veal/fish classics dominate — pork-free ordering is easy (no halal cert, alcohol served). Smart-casual, handles multi-generation tables well. Book several days ahead. $$$

places.name: Den Gyldene Freden
places.near: Österlånggatan; metro Gamla Stan 6 min
places.order: Meatballs of veal/beef (confirm mix); butter-fried herring; poached cod; seasonal game
places.famousFor: Open since 1722 and owned by the Swedish Academy — one of the world's oldest restaurants in an unchanged Old Town cellar where Nobel jurors have dined for centuries
places.goodToKnow: Historic atmosphere the whole family will remember; menu has pork dishes but fish and beef/veal classics are always on. Book ~1 week ahead for dinner. $$$

places.name: Meatballs for the People
places.near: Nytorgsgatan in SoFo; metro Medborgarplatsen 8 min
places.order: Beef, veal, reindeer or salmon/vegan balls with cream sauce and lingon (classic mix and boar contain pork — the menu labels each meat clearly); toast skagen
places.famousFor: The meatball temple of SoFo — 10+ ball varieties from beef and reindeer to boar and vegan, casual and fun; a family crowd-pleaser
places.goodToKnow: Each ball's meat is labelled, so ordering pork-free (beef/reindeer/elk/salmon/vegan) is genuinely easy — best meatball option in town for this family. Very kid-friendly; book or come early, small room. $$

places.name: Operakällaren
places.near: Operahuset at Karl XII:s torg; metro Kungsträdgården 3 min
places.order: Tasting or à la carte: turbot, langoustine and veal courses; the dessert trolley is legendary
places.notes: Visit Stockholm classic restaurants; iconic despite formality
places.famousFor: Sweden's grandest dining room inside the Royal Opera House — gilded 1895 interiors, Nobel-level service; the iconic special-occasion address since 1787
places.goodToKnow: Formal tasting-menu house — better as a parents-only or with the two eldest; kitchen accommodates no-pork with advance notice (alcohol-paired, no halal cert). Jacket recommended; book 2-3 weeks ahead. $$$$

places.name: Bakfickan
places.near: Jakobs Torg side of the Opera House; metro Kungsträdgården 3 min
places.order: Meatballs with cream sauce (confirm mix), butter-fried herring, toast skagen, daily fish special
places.famousFor: Operakällaren's tiny 'back pocket' — a horseshoe counter serving the same kitchen's husmanskost at a third of the price; a beloved Stockholm secret since 1904
places.goodToKnow: No reservations — counter seats only, so best for a split-group lunch rather than all 7 at once. Fish specials make pork-free easy. Great value for the pedigree. $$

places.name: Ulla Winbladh
places.near: Tram 7 to Skansen/Liljevalchs, 2 min walk; ferry to Allmänna Gränd 8 min
places.order: Ulla's butter-fried herring; salmon with dill-stewed potatoes; meatballs (ask mix); seasonal chanterelle toast
places.famousFor: Historic wärdshus in an 1897 World's Fair steam-bakery building beside Skansen — leafy summer terrace and proper Swedish cooking; welcomes 'royalty and families with children' alike
places.goodToKnow: Explicitly family-friendly with a lovely terrace — ideal Djurgården lunch between Skansen/Gröna Lund and the museums. Fish classics = easy pork-free. Book for summer weekend lunch. $$$

places.name: Ekstedt
places.near: Humlegårdsgatan; metro Östermalmstorg 3 min
places.order: Set menu (~6 courses): hay-flamed scallop, fire-baked fish, juniper-smoked desserts — kitchen tailors around no-pork with advance notice
places.famousFor: Niklas Ekstedt's one-star since 2013 — no electricity in the kitchen, everything cooked over birch fire, cast iron and smoke; theatrical and primal
places.goodToKnow: Tasting-menu format but shorter and more theatrical than most — the open fire genuinely entertains teens; better without the youngest two. Flag no-pork when booking (they adapt). Book 3-4 weeks ahead. $$$$

places.name: Aira
places.near: Biskopsvägen on south Djurgården; taxi or tram 7 + 12 min walk
places.order: Set menu: langoustine/browned butter, potato with vendace roe, turbot courses — pork-free adaptation with notice
places.notes: Michelin Guide 2025 (2 stars since 2023); Time Out/Amitylux
places.famousFor: Tommy Myllymäki's two-star glass pavilion on the Djurgården shoreline — langoustine in browned butter and wagyu with truffle, with the water right outside
places.goodToKnow: The city's most beautiful fine-dining setting — seafood-heavy menu adapts well to no-pork (mention at booking). Long tasting format: parents + eldest rather than all 7. Book 3-4 weeks out for August. $$$$

places.name: ergo.
places.near: Artillerigatan; metro Östermalmstorg 5 min
places.order: Tasting menu; seafood-forward courses — advise no-pork when reserving
places.famousFor: Stockholm's newest star (2025) — chef Petter Johansson (ex-Frantzén, Per Se) cooking precise, season-forward French-Nordic plates in an intimate Östermalm room
places.goodToKnow: Hot ticket after the 2025 star — book as far ahead as possible. Tasting-menu format, best for parents/older teens; kitchen handles no-pork with notice. $$$$

places.name: Sushi Sho
places.near: Upplandsgatan; metro Odenplan 6 min
places.order: Omakase only — Nordic-catch nigiri progression (all fish, naturally pork-free)
places.famousFor: Carl Ishizaki's tiny counter — Nordic fish through an Edomae lens, starred since 2016 and the hardest-working 20 seats in Swedish sushi
places.goodToKnow: All-fish omakase = perfect for the father; counter format suits 2-4 diners, not 7 — go as a split. Books out weeks ahead, set seating times. $$$$

places.name: Brasserie Astoria
places.near: Nybrogatan; metro Östermalmstorg 4 min
places.order: Whole grilled fish; vitello tonnato (veal); truffle pasta; the dessert trolley
places.famousFor: Björn Frantzén's big, glamorous all-day brasserie in the old Astoria cinema — the 3-star chef's cooking in shareable, family-accessible à la carte form
places.goodToKnow: The smart way to eat Frantzén-group cooking with kids — à la carte, buzzy, no dress code. Charcuterie/pork on menu but fish, veal and pasta make no-pork trivial. Book 1-2 weeks ahead for prime dinner slots. $$$

places.name: TAK
places.near: Brunkebergstorg, atop the At Six/Hobo block; metro T-Centralen 4 min
places.order: Robata-grilled fish; sashimi of Nordic catch; tempura; wagyu (beef)
places.famousFor: Frida Ronge's rooftop above Brunkebergstorg — Nordic produce with Japanese technique, plus one of the best terrace views over the city's rooftops
places.goodToKnow: Fish/beef-led Japanese menu = easy pork-free. Rooftop terrace magic on August evenings; teens will love it, fine for the younger ones early. Book ~1 week ahead, ask for terrace. $$$

places.name: Lilla Ego
places.near: Västmannagatan; metro Odenplan 5 min
places.order: 'Hopp i Havet' fish dish; charred cabbage; seasonal Nordic plates (menu labels meats; skip pork items)
places.famousFor: Stockholm's most beloved neighbourhood restaurant — two-chef kitchen, inventive Nordic food with Asian accents; tables vanish within minutes of release each month
places.goodToKnow: Tables released 1 month ahead and gone fast — set a reminder mid-July for the August dates; walk-in bar seats are the backup. Fish-strong menu, pork avoidable. Relaxed room, fine with older kids. $$$

places.name: Woodstockholm
places.near: Mosebacke Torg; metro Slussen 6 min uphill
places.order: Monthly themed menu; fish and vegetable courses (menu changes — flag no-pork and they steer you)
places.famousFor: Bib Gourmand bistro-with-wine-bar on leafy Mosebacke Torg — themed menus that change monthly, furniture by Swedish designers, cult local following
places.goodToKnow: Bib Gourmand value; small quirky room best for parents + teens. Menu rotates monthly so confirm pork-free options when booking (~1 week ahead). Near Södermalm viewpoints for an evening stroll. $$$

places.name: Kagges
places.near: Lilla Nygatan; metro Gamla Stan 4 min
places.order: 4-course 'let the kitchen choose' or à la carte small plates; signature dip-and-flatbread; fish crudo (no-pork easily arranged)
places.famousFor: Two young chefs' snug Old Town room — seasonal Swedish small plates that earned a cult following and Michelin-guide attention; the best modern meal in Gamla Stan
places.goodToKnow: Small room — for 7 you'll need an early booking or split tables; relaxed and welcoming. Share-plate format makes dietary steering easy. Book 1-2 weeks ahead. $$$

places.name: Babette
places.near: Roslagsgatan; metro Tekniska Högskolan 7 min
places.order: Margherita or seasonal veg pizzas (some pies use pork salumi — plenty don't); raw fish small plates
places.famousFor: Industry insiders' hangout — crisp sourdough pizzas and clever small plates; where Stockholm's chefs eat on their night off
places.goodToKnow: Mostly walk-in culture with some bookable tables — go early with kids. Pizza menu clearly lists toppings so pork is easy to dodge. Casual, great value for the quality. $$

places.name: Ärla
places.near: Danderydsgatan near Humlegården; metro Östermalmstorg/Tekniska Högskolan ~8 min
places.order: Seasonal fish mains; vegetable-forward starters (short menu — confirm no-pork when booking)
places.notes: Michelin Nordic 2025 — new Bib Gourmand; Tripadvisor address
places.famousFor: New Bib Gourmand 2025 — Patric Blomquist and Lotta Karlsson's open-kitchen bistro where the line between dining room and stove disappears; unfussy, flavour-packed cooking
places.goodToKnow: 2025's value story in Östermalm — Bib Gourmand pricing for near-starred cooking. Small; book ahead and mention no-pork. Best with older kids. $$$

places.name: Restaurang Hantverket
places.near: Sturegatan by Humlegården; metro Östermalmstorg 5 min
places.order: Sharing menu of Swedish small plates; fish and vegetable dishes labelled clearly; famous soft flatbread with brown butter
places.famousFor: Stefan Ekengren's craft-driven kitchen — modern Swedish share plates with serious technique but a relaxed, generous room; a locals' favourite for groups
places.goodToKnow: Roomy and group-friendly — one of the easier quality tables for all 7 together. Share-plate menu makes pork-free navigation simple. Book ~1 week ahead. $$$

places.name: Oaxen Slip
places.near: Beckholmsvägen past Gröna Lund; tram 7 + 8 min walk or Djurgården ferry
places.order: Fish of the day; mussels; inventive vegetarian plates; lamb (some sausage/charcuterie is pork — menu is clear)
places.famousFor: Magnus Ek's casual sister to Oaxen Krog — vintage boats hang from the triple-height boathouse ceiling by the Djurgården shipyard; Bib-level Nordic bistro cooking
places.goodToKnow: Perfect family lunch after Gröna Lund/Vasa — kids love the boats overhead. Strong fish + vegetarian range for pork-free. Book for summer weekends; terrace seats by the water. $$$

places.name: Rosendals Trädgård
places.near: Tram 7 to Bellmansro then 8 min walk through the park
places.order: Garden lunch plate (usually fish/veg options); wood-oven sourdough and cardamom buns; own-harvest salads
places.famousFor: Biodynamic garden café in the royal Djurgården orchards — wood-oven bakery, greenhouse dining and lunches picked metres away; a Stockholm summer ritual
places.goodToKnow: Daytime only, no reservations — self-serve under fruit trees; hugely kid-friendly (run-around space). Veg/fish-forward menu, pork rare. Cash-free. $$

places.name: Tosto
places.near: Sturegatan 6 at Stureplan; metro Östermalmstorg 3 min
places.order: Hand-rolled pasta (fish/veg sauces); aged fish dishes; beef mains (house charcuterie is largely pork — skip that section)
places.notes: thatsup new-restaurants + Time Out 2026 hot list
places.famousFor: One of 2025's hottest openings — chef Max Duhs' free-thinking Italian behind Sperling & Co: hand-rolled pasta daily, in-house aging and charcuterie, Stureplan buzz
places.goodToKnow: Buzzy new-opening energy — book well ahead (hottest-table lists 2025-26). Charcuterie programme is pork-heavy but pastas and fish deliver pork-free. Evening scene suits parents + teens. $$$

places.name: Chingu KBBQ & Sojubar
places.near: Mäster Samuelsgatan; metro Hötorget/T-Centralen 4 min
places.order: Beef bulgogi and galbi short rib on the table grill; bibimbap; Korean fried chicken (pork belly is a menu staple — order the beef/chicken sets)
places.famousFor: 2025 opening bringing proper Seoul-style table grilling to central Stockholm — socialise around the grill upstairs, Korean street food and cocktails below
places.goodToKnow: Table-grill format is a hit with kids/teens; beef and chicken options make pork easy to avoid (pork belly on menu — just don't order it). Book for weekend evenings. $$-$$$

places.name: Beirut Café
places.near: Nybrogatan; metro Östermalmstorg 3 min
places.order: Grand meze spread (hummus, moutabal, fattoush, kibbeh); charcoal-grilled lamb and chicken mashawi; fresh juices
places.notes: beirutcafe.se + Tripadvisor halal reviews + viewstockholm guide
places.famousFor: Stockholm's benchmark Lebanese since 1999 — meze 'is a religion' for regulars; chefs from the Levant, with a second counter inside Östermalms Saluhall
places.goodToKnow: Meals are halal per the restaurant and reviewers — the one table on this list where the whole family orders freely; big vegetarian meze range too. Group-friendly; book for 7. $$

places.name: Lebanon Meza Lounge
places.near: Norrmalmstorg; metro Östermalmstorg/Kungsträdgården 4 min
places.order: 15-dish meze tasting menu; grilled halloumi; lamb and chicken skewers; knefe for dessert
places.notes: Tripadvisor top Lebanese Stockholm; viewstockholm guide
places.famousFor: Upscale Levantine at Norrmalmstorg — four composed tasting menus of 15 hot-and-cold meze; the polished end of Stockholm's Lebanese scene
places.goodToKnow: Naturally pork-free Levantine kitchen (alcohol served; confirm halal sourcing directly if it matters). Meze-sharing format is ideal for 7 people. Book a few days ahead. $$$

places.name: Farang
places.near: Tulegatan; metro Rådmansgatan 5 min
places.order: (When open) sharing menus: fish larb, whole crispy fish, beef cheek curry — pork items labelled
places.famousFor: Stockholm's flagship SE-Asian kitchen in a dramatic former power station — CAUTION: closed for renovation from 1 June 2026, reopening announced for fall 2026, so likely NOT open during a 14-19 Aug visit
places.goodToKnow: VERIFY BEFORE PLANNING: own site says closed June 1 2026, reopening fall/September 2026 — check farang.se close to the trip; included only so it isn't booked by mistake or missed if reopening early. $$$

places.name: Mr Cake (Rådmansgatan)
places.near: 1 min from Rådmansgatan metro; second branch at Regeringsgatan 57
places.order: Red velvet croissant (signature); cruffins; cheesecake; American-style layer cakes; brunch plates
places.notes: mrcake.se + Yelp/Tripadvisor (Rådmansgatan 12, Roy Fares, red velvet croissant)
places.famousFor: TV pastry chef Roy Fares' viral dessert café — home of the red velvet croissant and jaw-dropping American-style cakes
places.goodToKnow: Very Instagram-famous — expect a queue at weekend brunch; big portions ideal for sharing among kids

places.name: Snö Gelateria
places.near: Opposite Vasaparken on Odengatan 92, 5 min walk from Odenplan metro
places.order: Pistachio and hazelnut gelato; seasonal sorbets; gelato cakes
places.notes: snogelateria.se + thatsup.se gelato guide (Odengatan 92, Swedish champions)
places.famousFor: Swedish ice-cream champions 2016 & 2018 — gelato master Kenneth Erlandsson's authentic Sardinian-style gelato, with summer queues down Odengatan
places.goodToKnow: Perfect pairing with Vasaparken's big playground across the street; expect a line on warm August evenings

places.name: Kennys Gelato (Djurgården)
places.near: On Djurgårdsstrand 15 near the Djurgården bridge — on the walk between Vasa Museum/Skansen and Gröna Lund; tram 7 or Djurgården ferry
places.order: Award-winning strawberry sorbet; daily-made gelato scoops; celebrity-collab flavours
places.famousFor: Sweden's ice-cream master 2022 Kenny Mattsson — twelve fresh gelato/sorbet flavours made each morning, some created with Swedish celebrities
places.goodToKnow: Ideal treat stop on a Djurgården museum day; more branches at Sergelterrassen, Kungsbroplan and Sickla

places.name: Stockholms Glasshus
places.near: Birkagatan 8 in Birkastan, ~7 min walk from St Eriksplan metro
places.order: A 3-scoop cup mixing Swedish and Italian styles; sorbets; ice-cream cake to take away
places.famousFor: Vasastan classic making artisanal gelato and sherbet from scratch since 1986 — around 48 flavours including sugar-free options, plus ice-cream cakes
places.goodToKnow: Huge flavour range means every one of 7 family members finds something; sugar-free and lactose-free options

places.name: StikkiNikki (Götgatan)
places.near: Götgatan 46, 3 min from Medborgarplatsen or Slussen metro on Södermalm's main street
places.order: Salted caramel gelato; vegan chocolate sorbet; fresh waffle cone
places.famousFor: The pink organic gelaterias of Stockholm — gelato made fresh daily from mostly organic ingredients with a standout vegan/lactose-free range
places.goodToKnow: Best pick if anyone in the family is lactose-intolerant; several branches around the city (also Mariatorget)

places.name: Pärlans Konfektyr
places.near: Nytorgsgatan 38 by Nytorget square, ~8 min walk from Medborgarplatsen metro
places.order: Mixed box of salted liquorice/vanilla/chocolate caramels; caramel sauce jar; summer ice cream with caramel drizzle
places.notes: parlanskonfektyr.se + Apple Maps listing (Nytorgsgatan 38, coords from Apple Maps)
places.famousFor: 1930s-styled artisan caramel workshop where staff in vintage dress hand-wrap butter-soft caramels in the window — a SoFo icon; also scoops luxury ice cream in summer
places.goodToKnow: Caramel boxes are the best edible souvenir to take back to the UAE (they keep well); watch the caramel-making through the window with the kids; Nytorget playground opposite

places.name: Ritorno
places.near: Odengatan by Vasaparken, near Odenplan metro/commuter station
places.order: Prinsesstårta or dammsugare with coffee; big classic pastry counter
places.famousFor: Time-capsule 1950s-60s konditori loved by generations of Stockholmers; took 3rd place in the city's 2025 semla test
places.goodToKnow: Open late for a konditori (to ~21:00 midweek); roomy retro booths suit a big family; opposite Vasaparken playground

places.name: Chokladfabriken
places.near: Renstiernas gata on eastern Södermalm, buses from Slussen or ~10 min walk from Medborgarplatsen metro
places.order: Praline selection box; dark hot chocolate; chocolate cake in the café corner
places.famousFor: Stockholm's flagship bean-to-praline chocolate factory — flagship store on Renstiernas gata houses the actual factory plus a café; pralines, truffles, cakes and famous hot chocolate
places.goodToKnow: Everything handmade on site; second branch at Regeringsgatan 58 in the city centre if Södermalm doesn't fit the route

places.name: Ejes Choklad
places.near: Erik Dahlbergsgatan on Gärdet, short walk from Gärdet metro (red line)
places.order: Fresh pralines; their legendary chokladbollar and seasonal truffles
places.notes: ejeschoklad.se history page + Thatsup/Culture Trip chocolate guides
places.famousFor: Stockholm's oldest family-run chocolate factory and appointed chocolate purveyor to the Swedish Royal Court — everything made on premises with no preservatives
places.goodToKnow: A shop/factory rather than a café — combine with a Djurgården/Gärdet day; royal-warrant story is a nice hook for the kids

places.name: MR CAKE
places.near: Rådmansgatan metro (green line) is right by the flagship
places.order: Red velvet croissant; Devil's chocolate cake; family pancake brunch; cheesecake slice
places.famousFor: Pastry-star Roy Fares' dessert temple (since 2017) — red velvet croissants, over-the-top cakes, cheesecakes, pancakes and 'semmelcronuts'; the most Instagrammed dessert café in town
places.goodToKnow: Full brunch/breakfast menu too, so it works as a family meal not just dessert; second central outlet in the MOOD gallery (Regeringsgatan)

places.name: Socker Sucker
places.near: Upper Drottninggatan, ~5 min walk from Rådmansgatan metro
places.order: Cube croissant in whatever flavour is on; laminated pastries; plated-style petit gâteaux
places.famousFor: The viral 2020s star of Stockholm baking — award-winning pastry chefs Bedros Kabranian & Frida Bäcke reinvent Swedish classics; TikTok-famous cube/square croissants (tiramisu croissant etc.)
places.goodToKnow: Popular flavours sell out — go before noon; small space, expect a short queue at weekends

places.name: K-märkt Garnisonen
places.cuisine: Modern Swedish — patisserie by star pastry chef
places.near: Garnisonen building, Karlavägen 100; Karlaplan metro then short walk, or bus 4
places.order: Whatever is on the dessert counter — plated pastries, cakes and the famous dessert buffet when running
places.famousFor: Run by celebrity pastry chef Daniel Roos (of Nobel Banquet dessert fame) — its in-house patisserie and legendary pay-by-weight dessert spread made it 2nd in Stockholm's 2025 semla test
places.goodToKnow: Primarily a weekday lunch operation — check current opening days/dessert-buffet schedule online before making the trip in August

places.name: Gräddnos Gelateria
places.near: Rörstrandsgatan café street, ~5 min walk from S:t Eriksplan metro
places.order: Sicilian pistachio; seasonal fruit sorbets; brioche con gelato if offered
places.famousFor: Family-run gelateria with roots in Catania, Sicily — gelato made on Rörstrandsgatan from Italian and Swedish ingredients; a Vasastan neighbourhood favourite on the city's best-gelato lists
places.goodToKnow: Pair with Lillebrors Bageri two doors down for a bun-then-gelato double stop on the same street

places.name: Kungsholmens Glassfabrik
places.near: Pipersgatan, ~5 min walk from Rådhuset metro (blue line), near City Hall
places.order: Salted caramel; seasonal Swedish-berry flavours; kids' scoops in sugar cones
places.famousFor: Beloved small-batch 'ice-cream factory' on Kungsholmen repeatedly cited among Stockholm's very best scoops — long queues on warm evenings are part of the ritual
places.goodToKnow: Perfect after a City Hall / Rålambshovsparken visit; mostly takeaway — eat by the water

places.name: Glassverkstan Skeppsholmen
places.near: Slupskjulsvägen on Skeppsholmen — walk across Skeppsholmsbron from Nationalmuseum, or ferry from Slussen/Gamla Stan
places.order: Fresh raspberry sorbet; pistachio; whatever the day's blackboard says
places.famousFor: Hand-made gelato in an old bakery on museum island Skeppsholmen — fresh raspberries, real pistachio and lemon, flavours changing with season and the makers' mood
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal/weather-dependent opening — ideal mid-August; perfect stop between Moderna Museet and the Vasa/Djurgården ferry

places.name: Mr Churros Gamla Stan
places.near: Västerlånggatan, Gamla Stan's main lane — 3 min from Gamla Stan metro
places.order: Classic churros with chocolate dip; Nutella-strawberry waffle; churros with ice cream for the kids
places.famousFor: Fresh-fried Spanish churros in the middle of the Old Town — crisp outside, soft inside, rated ~4.6/5 by hundreds of visitors; also does loaded Belgian-style waffles
places.goodToKnow: Casual grab-and-go — ideal energy stop mid Old Town walk; second city-centre branch exists

places.name: Rosendals Trädgårdskafé
places.near: Rosendal on Djurgården — tram 7 towards Waldemarsudde then 10 min walk, or a stroll from Skansen
places.order: Daily cakes ('grandmother classics'), cardamom bun, seasonal berry tarts; garden-grown lemonade
places.notes: rosendalstradgard.se + visitstockholm.com + White Guide café listing
places.famousFor: Magical greenhouse-garden café in a biodynamic orchard on Djurgården; cakes and buns baked in the wood-fired oven next door, eaten under apple trees
places.goodToKnow: Best family fika setting in Stockholm in August — kids roam the gardens; combine with Skansen/Vasa/Gröna Lund on a Djurgården day; mostly outdoor, weather-dependent

places.name: Söder Marley Cafe
places.order: Marley Coffee brew with a slice of fresh cake
places.famousFor: Cosy Bob Marley-themed café on Sodermalm serving organic Marley Coffee
places.goodToKnow: ~400m from Medborgarplatsen metro; serves sandwiches and daily soup

places.name: Vete-Katten
places.near: 2 min walk from Hötorget metro; entrances on Kungsgatan 55, Klara Norra Kyrkogata 26 and Gamla Brogatan 30 (wheelchair accessible)
places.order: Prinsesstårta (princess cake); kanelbulle; kardemummabulle; a classic 'fika' coffee + pastry combo
places.famousFor: Stockholm's most beloved fika institution since 1928 — a warren of old-fashioned rooms serving princess cake, kanelbullar and semlor
places.goodToKnow: Big enough for a family of 7 (many rooms + inner courtyard); no alcohol focus — pure pastry café; can queue at weekend peak

places.name: Sturekatten
places.near: 3 min walk from Östermalmstorg metro, just off Stureplan
places.order: Kanelbulle; apple pie with vanilla sauce; princess cake slice with tea or coffee
places.famousFor: Time-capsule 1940s café (opened 1941) in an 18th-century townhouse, furnished with the founding sisters' own antiques over several floors
places.goodToKnow: Feels like fika in a grandmother's parlour; multiple small rooms — go before noon for space for a big family

places.name: Sundbergs Konditori
places.near: 5 min walk from Gamla Stan metro, on Järntorget square at the south end of the Old Town
places.order: Kanelbulle; punschrulle; a classic pastry with coffee served from the copper samovar
places.famousFor: Stockholm's oldest café — founded 1785 by royal pastry chef Gustaf Adolf Sundberg, still on Järntorget square in the Old Town
places.goodToKnow: Tiny and atmospheric — better as a quick heritage stop than a long family sit-down; pairs well with an Old Town walk

places.name: Tössebageriet
places.near: Short walk from Karlaplan metro along Karlavägen
places.order: Kanelbulle; semla (in season); wienerbröd; cake slices from the long counter
places.famousFor: Classic konditori at the same Karlavägen address since 1920; huge pastry counter and one of the city's most awarded semla/bun makers
places.goodToKnow: Traditional café feel; take-away boxes make a good picnic for Djurgården nearby

places.name: Wienercaféet
places.near: 2 min from Östermalmstorg metro; corner of Biblioteksgatan 6-8 / Mäster Samuelsgatan, on the main shopping street
places.order: Afternoon tea stand; princess cake; pistachio croissant; hot chocolate
places.famousFor: Grand café anno 1904 on Biblioteksgatan, revived as an opulent patisserie famous for afternoon tea and picture-perfect cakes
places.goodToKnow: Elegant, popular for celebrations — book ahead for a family of 7; ideal combined with Biblioteksgatan shopping

places.name: Café Saturnus
places.near: Short walk from Rådmansgatan or Tekniska Högskolan metro, near Humlegården park
places.order: The famous giant kanelbulle or kardemummabulle (shareable!); breakfast pancakes; café au lait in a bowl
places.notes: Yelp + Tripadvisor reviews (Eriksbergsgatan 6, biggest bun in Stockholm)
places.famousFor: The giant cinnamon and cardamom buns — at least twice the size of a normal kanelbulle — in an artsy French-style café where celebrities linger
places.goodToKnow: One giant bun easily feeds two kids; Humlegården playground is a 5-min walk for after-fika run-around

places.name: Lillebrors Bageri
places.near: 5 min walk from St Eriksplan metro along café street Rörstrandsgatan
places.order: Kardemummabulle (the signature); kanelbulle on Cinnamon Bun Day season; fresh sourdough loaf
places.famousFor: Repeatedly voted Stockholm's best kardemummabulle — small artisan bakery baking buns all day and selling them warm over the counter
places.goodToKnow: Hole-in-the-wall with few seats — take buns to Vasaparken; often sells the cardamom bun only, arrive before mid-afternoon

places.name: Fabrique Stenugnsbageri (Rosenlundsgatan)
places.near: Short walk from Mariatorget or Zinkensdamm metro
places.order: Kanelbulle; kardemummabulle; sourdough bread; cheese sandwich on levain
places.famousFor: The original of Sweden's famous stone-oven bakery chain (now also in London/NYC) — benchmark kanelbullar and kardemummabullar
places.goodToKnow: Many branches across the city (Gamla Stan Lilla Nygatan 12, Nybrogatan 6 etc.) — handy fallback wherever you are

places.name: Bröd & Salt (Regeringsgatan)
places.near: Central branch at Regeringsgatan 47 (by Mood Gallerian), 5 min from T-Centralen; branches all over town
places.order: Kardemummabulle; kanelbulle; sourdough sandwiches; fresh pizza slices
places.famousFor: Much-loved Stockholm bakery chain whose juicy kardemummabullar are rated 'to die for'; everything baked traditionally in their own Västberga bakery
places.goodToKnow: Reliable, roomy and family-friendly; you're never far from a branch (Odenplan, Södermalm etc.)

places.name: Skeppsbro Bageri
places.near: On the Skeppsbron quay (Tullhus 1), 5 min walk from Gamla Stan metro or Slussen; great during an Old Town waterfront stroll
places.order: Kanelbulle with deep golden glaze; kardemummabulle; organic sourdough
places.notes: Visit Stockholm + thatsup.co.uk (won best bun competition; Tullhus 1)
places.famousFor: Recent winner of Stockholm's best-bun competition — organic bakery in an old customs house on the Old Town quay that mills its own flour
places.goodToKnow: Outdoor quay-side tables with harbour views; combine with ferry to Djurgården from nearby Slussen

places.name: Chokladkoppen
places.near: 3 min walk from Gamla Stan metro, on the main Stortorget square by the Nobel Museum
places.order: Hot chocolate in a bowl; giant kanelbulle; white-chocolate cheesecake; kladdkaka
places.famousFor: The postcard café on Stortorget — hot chocolate served in bowls and plate-sized cinnamon buns inside a 1520s house with a candlelit vaulted cellar
places.goodToKnow: Even Jamie Oliver raves about the hot chocolate; outdoor square seating in August; small inside — split the family between terrace and cellar if busy

places.name: Grillska Husets Konditori
places.near: 3 min from Gamla Stan metro, on Stortorget next to the Nobel Museum
places.order: Kanelbulle; daily cake; shrimp/egg smörgås for a light lunch
places.famousFor: Café-konditori in a 17th-century merchant house on Stortorget with a hidden inner courtyard and rooftop terrace; run by Stockholm City Mission — every fika supports charity
places.goodToKnow: The quiet courtyard is a great escape from Old Town crowds with kids; everything baked in-house from scratch

places.name: Under Kastanjen
places.near: 5 min walk from Gamla Stan metro; on tiny Brända Tomten square at Kindstugatan/Själagårdsgatan
places.order: Kanelbulle under the chestnut tree; kladdkaka; soup + bread lunch
places.famousFor: Fairytale café under the big chestnut tree on Brända Tomten, the Old Town's prettiest little square — a classic photo stop with home-baked buns
places.goodToKnow: Go daytime for the café/bakery side (it runs a bistro-bar in the evening); outdoor square tables are lovely in August

places.name: Drop Coffee
places.near: 2 min from Mariatorget metro
places.order: Pour-over of a single-origin; flat white; cinnamon bun from their pastry counter
places.notes: dropcoffee.com + adayin.se Stockholm coffee guide (Wollmar Yxkullsgatan 10)
places.famousFor: Stockholm's flag-bearer specialty roaster — multi-award-winning light roasts, with co-owner Joanna Alm ranked among the world's top roasters
places.goodToKnow: Minimalist and compact — best for a coffee-nerd stop while others browse Södermalm; beans make good gifts to bring home

places.name: Johan & Nyström Konceptbutik
places.near: 3 min from Mariatorget metro on pedestrian-friendly Swedenborgsgatan
places.order: Slow-brew tasting flight; cappuccino; chai for the kids; buy beans/tea to take home
places.notes: adayin.se + Falstaff top-5 coffee bars (Swedenborgsgatan 7, roasting since 2004)
places.famousFor: Pioneers of Sweden's third-wave coffee since 2004 — cosy flagship concept store on Swedenborgsgatan with brew bar and every gadget imaginable
places.goodToKnow: Roomier than most specialty cafés — sofas and long tables suit a group; 100m from Drop Coffee so you can compare

places.name: Café Pascal
places.near: 1 min from Odenplan metro/commuter station
places.order: Cardamom bun; toasted cheese sandwich; cortado on house-roasted beans; breakfast plates
places.notes: adayin.se + campervansweden.com guides (Norrtullsgatan 4, DN best café)
places.famousFor: Named Stockholm's best café by Dagens Nyheter — own-roasted coffee, own-baked pastries and legendary grilled cheese sandwiches
places.goodToKnow: Busy at weekend brunch; two sister branches in town if full; near Vasaparken playground

places.name: Mellqvist Kaffebar
places.near: 3 min from St Eriksplan metro, at the start of Rörstrandsgatan's café row (Lillebrors is up the same street)
places.order: Cappuccino; breakfast roll; blueberry pie
places.famousFor: Stockholm café institution on Rörstrandsgatan — Stieg Larsson's real-life hangout, featured in the Millennium trilogy (he wrote pages of it here)
places.goodToKnow: Fun literary stop for Millennium fans; visit in the daytime — it shifts to evening drinks later

places.name: Pom & Flora (Södermalm)
places.near: SoFo branch at Bondegatan 64, ~8 min walk from Medborgarplatsen metro; also at Odengatan 39 and Borgmästargatan 11
places.order: Rye porridge with berries; açaí bowl; sourdough with avocado & egg; fresh juices
places.famousFor: Stockholm's viral all-day-breakfast café (since 2013) — pretty porridge, açaí and sourdough plates that fill every local Instagram feed
places.goodToKnow: First-come first-served (no bookings) — go early with 7 people; excellent no-alcohol juice/smoothie list

places.name: Rosendals Trädgård Café
places.near: Rosendalsterrassen 12 on Djurgården — tram 7 to Bellmansro then a 10-min walk, or a pleasant stroll from Skansen past Rosendal Palace
places.order: Kanelbulle from the wood oven; seasonal garden salad or soup lunch; rhubarb/berry cakes; garden-herb lemonade
places.famousFor: Fika among apple orchards and flower beds in a biodynamic garden on Djurgården — pastries from the on-site wood-fired bakery, tables in the greenhouse or under fruit trees
places.goodToKnow: Kids can roam the gardens and farm shop; largely outdoor — perfect for August; weekend queues move fast

places.name: Flickorna Helin (Skånska Gruvan)
places.cuisine: Café & bakery in a heritage building
places.near: Rosendalsvägen 14 on Djurgården — tram 7 or ferry, on the walk between Vasa Museum and Rosendal
places.order: Big home-baked buns and cakes; shrimp sandwich; hot chocolate after a museum visit
places.famousFor: Café-bakery inside Skånska Gruvan, a fairytale tower building from the 1897 Stockholm World's Fair, right on the Djurgården waterfront promenade
places.goodToKnow: Generous outdoor seating with water views — easy with strollers and a group of 7; casual counter service

places.name: Gröna Lund
places.near: Djurgarden, Stockholm; take the tram (line 7) or the Djurgarden ferry from Slussen.
places.order: The Insane and Jetline coasters and the Eclipse swing tower; catch a live concert in season.
places.famousFor: Grona Lund, Stockholm's historic waterfront amusement park on Djurgarden, known for thrill coasters packed onto a compact island site and summer concerts.
places.goodToKnow: Open seasonally (spring to autumn); famous as a summer concert venue with major acts. · Kids: Height limits for 5yo

places.name: Vasa Museum (Vasamuseet)
places.near: Djurgården; tram 7 or Djurgården ferry; paid parking on Djurgården fills early in summer — arrive at 8:30 opening
places.famousFor: The world's only preserved 17th-century warship — the 1628 Vasa, salvaged 95% intact — Scandinavia's most visited museum
places.goodToKnow: Aug 2026: open daily 08:30-18:00; adults 240 SEK (May-Sep rate), 0-18 FREE — great value for a family of 7; cash-free; allow 2h · Kids: All ages

places.name: Skansen Open-Air Museum
places.near: Djurgården; tram 7 from Norrmalmstorg or drive + paid Djurgården parking; combine with Vasa/ABBA same day
places.notes: Source: skansen.se opening hours & prices page
places.famousFor: World's oldest open-air museum (1891): historic Sweden in miniature plus Nordic zoo with moose, bears, wolves and reindeer
places.goodToKnow: Summer 2026: ~10:00-18:00; adults ~220-245 SEK online (cheaper than gate ~260-305), kids 0-15 FREE but must pre-book a free child ticket on skansen.se; half-day visit · Kids: All ages; stroller-friendly

places.name: Junibacken (Astrid Lindgren story house)
places.near: Next to Vasa Museum on Djurgården; tram 7 or ferry; same parking as Vasa
places.famousFor: Pippi Longstocking's world: the flying Story Train over miniature Astrid Lindgren scenes, Villa Villekulla play house and daily theatre
places.goodToKnow: Jul-Aug opens 08:30 (until 18:00 selected days); tickets from 160 SEK, cheapest online; theatres included but pre-book; ideal for the 5- and 9-year-old, Story Train queues on Saturdays · Kids: Best 5-11; teens find it young

places.name: ABBA The Museum
places.near: Djurgårdsvägen next to Gröna Lund; tram 7 or Djurgården ferry from Slussen/Gamla Stan
places.famousFor: Interactive pop museum where you sing, dance and perform on stage as the 5th member of ABBA — big hit with teens
places.goodToKnow: Jun-Aug 2026: 10:00-20:00 daily; 2026 prices: adult 249-329 SEK, child 7-15 100-120 SEK, 0-6 free, family 549-749 SEK; book a time slot online; ~1.5-2h · Kids: Best 9+; adults & teens

places.name: Royal Palace of Stockholm (Kungliga slottet)
places.near: Top of Gamla Stan at Slottsbacken; metro Gamla Stan then 7-min walk; no parking at palace — use Slussen/city garages
places.famousFor: One of Europe's largest palaces (600+ rooms) — royal apartments, the Treasury with crown jewels and Gustav III's museum of antiquities
places.goodToKnow: Children 6 and under free; ticket covers royal apartments + Treasury; check kungligaslotten.se admission page the week you travel (prices adjust yearly); combine with the free guard ceremony at 12:15 · Kids: Best 9+

places.name: Changing of the Royal Guard
places.near: Outer courtyard of the Royal Palace, Gamla Stan; arrive 30 min early for a front-row spot on the cobbles
places.famousFor: Daily 40-minute military ceremony with mounted parade and army band in the Royal Palace outer courtyard — free spectacle kids love
places.goodToKnow: FREE; daily through 31 Aug — 12:15 Mon-Sat, 13:15 Sundays/holidays; full parade with music marches through town before the ceremony · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages
places.address: Yttre borggården, Kungliga slottet, Gamla Stan, Stockholm

places.name: Stockholm City Hall Tower (Stadshuset)
places.near: Kungsholmen waterfront, 10-min walk from T-Centralen; tower entry by timed slots (27 tickets per slot)
places.notes: Source: stadshuset.stockholm City Hall Tower tickets page
places.famousFor: The 106 m brick tower with the Three Crowns — climb for the classic panorama over Gamla Stan and Riddarfjärden; home of the Nobel banquet
places.goodToKnow: Aug 1-22 2026 tower tours 09:00-16:20 (every 50 min); adult 100 SEK, kids 0-11 free with adult; tickets online 1 week ahead or same-day at 08:30 ticket office; ~40 min visit · Kids: Best 9+; 365 steps

places.name: Fotografiska Stockholm
places.near: Stadsgårdshamnen waterfront below Södermalm; 10-min walk from Slussen metro; paid parking nearby
places.famousFor: World-renowned photography museum in a 1906 industrial waterfront building — top-floor café has one of the best free views over the harbour
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00-23:00 — perfect late-evening option; adult 225 SEK, kids up to 12 free with adult, 13-16 always FREE; exhibitions rotate constantly · Kids: Best 9+; check exhibits

places.name: Tekniska museet + Wisdome (Science & Technology Museum)
places.near: Museivägen on Norra Djurgården (Gärdet); easy free-ish street parking compared to central Djurgården; bus 69 from city
places.famousFor: Sweden's national science museum: MegaMind interactive lab, Hyper Human, Dreamland and the Wisdome dome-cinema — hands-on heaven for kids
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 09:00-21:00 (rare late hours); students/pupils up to 19 just 60 SEK, under-6 free; Wisdome planetarium shows cost extra; suits all five kids' ages · Kids: All ages (hands-on)

places.name: Swedish Museum of Natural History + Cosmonova
places.near: Frescati, north of centre — easy drive with on-site parking, or metro to Universitetet
places.notes: Source: nrm.se opening hours & admission fees
places.famousFor: Dinosaurs, whales and Nordic wildlife plus Cosmonova — Sweden's biggest IMAX dome cinema/planetarium
places.goodToKnow: Exhibitions FREE for kids/students, adults 170 SEK; combo with one Cosmonova show: adult 260 SEK, child 100 SEK; closed Mondays — check summer hours before going; cashless · Kids: All ages

places.name: Drottningholm Palace (UNESCO)
places.area: Lovön, Ekerö (11 km W of Stockholm)
places.near: Easy 25-30 min DRIVE from central Stockholm via Drottningholmsvägen; parking by the entrance (~15 SEK/h; free near Slottsteater); or boat from City Hall
places.famousFor: The royal family's actual residence — Sweden's Versailles: UNESCO-listed 17th-century palace, baroque gardens, Chinese Pavilion and 18th-century court theatre
places.goodToKnow: May-Sep daily 10:00-17:00; palace ~130 SEK adult, under-7 free; the vast gardens are FREE — bring a picnic; Chinese Pavilion costs extra; half-day trip with the van · Kids: Gardens all; palace 9+

places.name: Fjäderholmarna Islands
places.near: No car access — 30-min ferry from Strandvägen/Nybrokajen (Strömma) or Slussen; boats every ~30 min in summer
places.famousFor: The archipelago in 30 minutes: craft studios, smokehouse restaurant, brewery, homemade ice cream and rocky swimming spots — easiest island taste for families
places.goodToKnow: Round trip roughly 170-195 SEK pp; eat at Rökeriet or Fjäderholmarnas Krog, ice cream at Systrarna Degen; go late afternoon for golden-hour photos back over the city · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (short boat hop)

places.name: Vaxholm & Kastellet Fortress
places.near: 35-45 min DRIVE via E18 then road 274; park 5-10 min outside the centre (harbour lots fill in summer); Kastellet shuttle boat every 20 min from berth 7
places.notes: Source: stromma.com Vaxholm guide + kastellet.com travel page
places.famousFor: 'Capital of the archipelago' — pastel wooden houses, harbour cafés, cinnamon-bun bakeries and the 1544 island fortress guarding the strait
places.goodToKnow: Best half-day car trip: stroll Hamngatan shops, swim off the cliffs at Batteriparken, fika at Hembygdsgården café; combine with scenic archipelago drive back · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages

places.name: Sigtuna Old Town (Sweden's oldest town)
places.near: 45-min DRIVE from Stockholm between E4 and E18 (20-30 min from Arlanda); park by the harbour and walk everywhere
places.famousFor: Founded AD 980: Sweden's oldest street Stora Gatan, pastel wooden houses, Viking runestones everywhere, church ruins and the tiny 1744 town hall
places.goodToKnow: FREE to wander; most runestone-rich town in the world; lakefront path along Mälaren with jetties for photos; cafés and Swedish design boutiques in the old workshops; pairs with Steninge Slott · Kids: All ages

places.name: Taxinge Slott — the 'Cake Castle' (Kakslottet)
places.near: ~55-min DRIVE via E20 towards Nykvarn on Lake Mälaren; free parking at the castle; can combine with Gripsholm/Mariefred
places.famousFor: Northern Europe's largest cake buffet — ~65 kinds of homemade cakes, pastries and sandwiches served in a lakeside castle
places.goodToKnow: May-Aug café daily 11:00-18:00; you pay per pastry you pick — kids love loading their own plates; castle lawns run down to the lake, very Instagrammable fika · Kids: All ages (cake buffet)

places.name: Gripsholm Castle & Mariefred
places.near: ~1h DRIVE via E20; parking by the castle meadow (watch for the royal deer herd); classic full or half-day trip
places.famousFor: Gustav Vasa's 1537 red-brick fairy-tale castle with round towers on Lake Mälaren, beside the idyllic wooden town of Mariefred with its steam railway
places.goodToKnow: Mid-May-mid-Sep daily 10:00-16:00; adult 170 SEK, child 85 SEK (2026); allow 1.5-2h inside; afterwards ice cream + heritage steam train in Mariefred; combines well with Taxinge cake castle · Kids: All ages

places.name: Artipelag
places.area: Hålludden, Gustavsberg, Värmdö (25 min E of Stockholm)
places.near: ~25-min scenic DRIVE via route 222 towards Gustavsberg; large free parking; ideal rainy-day + nature combo
places.famousFor: Spectacular art museum built into pine forest and cliffs by the archipelago water — world-class exhibitions plus free nature boardwalk trails
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 11:00-17:00; building, café, design shop and seaside nature trails FREE — you only pay for exhibitions; NOTE: closed to public 20 Aug 2026 (after your dates); buffet lunches at Bådan café · Kids: All ages (art + nature)

places.name: SkyView — Avicii Arena glass gondola
places.near: Avicii Arena at Globen, 10 min DRIVE south of centre; arena parking garages; metro Globen also easy
places.famousFor: Glass gondolas riding up the outside of the world's largest spherical building to 85 m for a panorama over all of Stockholm
places.goodToKnow: Adults 180 SEK, kids 5-12 140 SEK, 0-4 free; Mon-Fri 10-18, Sat-Sun 10-16; each gondola takes 12 — your family of 7 nearly fills one; ~20-min ride, book online · Kids: All ages (enclosed gondola)

places.name: Rålambshovsparken & Rålis Parklek
places.near: Western Kungsholmen under Västerbron bridge; 5-min walk from Fridhemsplan metro; street parking on Kungsholmen
places.famousFor: 'Rålis' — huge lakeside park with one of Stockholm's best playgrounds, sandy Mälaren beach, skatepark, climbing wall and kubb lawns
places.goodToKnow: FREE; Rålis Parklek playground covers all your kids' ages (staffed parklek concept); BBQ areas + beach volleyball; weekday mornings quietest in August · Kids: All ages (top playground)

places.name: Stockholm Culture Festival (Kulturfestivalen) 2026
places.area: Central Stockholm (Gamla Stan, Slussen, Skeppsbrokajen, Kungsträdgården)
places.near: Festival zones around Slottsbacken, Slussen and Skeppsbrokajen — all walkable from Gamla Stan; avoid driving into the center on festival evenings
places.famousFor: The city's biggest festival — 5 days of free concerts, dance, street art, food and kids' activities across central Stockholm's streets and quays
places.goodToKnow: 12-16 Aug 2026 — overlaps your first days (14-16 Aug)! Totally FREE, hundreds of events incl. dedicated children's program; 2026 areas: Catalan culture at Slottsbacken, waterside stages at Slussen; program published July 2026 · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (free events)

places.name: Nordiska museet (Nordic Museum)
places.near: Grand building at the Djurgården bridge, before Vasa Museum; tram 7 or short walk from Djurgårdsbron
places.famousFor: Cathedral-like renaissance palace on Djurgården telling 500 years of Nordic life — folk costumes, Sami culture, dollhouses and the giant Gustav Vasa statue
places.goodToKnow: Daily 10:00-17:00; adult 170 SEK, kids 3-18 75 SEK; the great hall is an Instagram favourite; quick 1h visit slots nicely before Vasa next door · Kids: All ages (kids' play zone)

places.name: The Viking Museum (Vikingaliv)
places.near: Djurgårdsvägen strip near ABBA Museum and Gröna Lund; tram 7 or Djurgården ferry
places.famousFor: Interactive Viking-age museum with costumed guides and 'Ragnfrid's Saga' — an 11-minute dark ride through a Viking story kids adore
places.goodToKnow: Adults 199 SEK, kids 7-15 159 SEK, 0-6 free, family (2+2) 599 SEK; compact 1-1.5h — good add-on between the big Djurgården museums; restaurant Glöd on site · Kids: All ages; ride dim for 5yo

places.name: Paradox Museum Stockholm
places.near: Sergelgatan pedestrian street by Hötorget metro, 5 min from T-Centralen; city-centre garages nearby
places.famousFor: 70+ optical-illusion exhibits — upside-down rooms, infinity tunnels and gravity tricks built for crazy family photos
places.goodToKnow: Sun-Thu 10-19, Fri-Sat 10-21; adults from 249 SEK, kids from 195 SEK, under-5 free; the most Instagrammable indoor hour in town — phone fully charged; book online to skip sell-outs · Kids: All ages (photo fun)

places.name: Fjärilshuset Haga Ocean (Butterfly & Shark House)
places.near: Inside Hagaparken royal park, 10-min DRIVE north of centre; parking at the park entrance
places.notes: Source: en.fjarilshuset.se tickets & opening hours pages
places.famousFor: Tropical greenhouses with free-flying butterflies plus one of Scandinavia's largest shark aquariums (30 m tank, 1.2 million litres)
places.goodToKnow: Typically ~10:00-17:00 (check en.fjarilshuset.se for Aug 2026); under-4 free; family of 7 pays per-person so budget ~900+ SEK; warm humid greenhouse — dress light; pairs with a Hagaparken picnic · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages; stroller-friendly

places.name: Hagaparken (Royal National City Park)
places.near: 10-min DRIVE north via Uppsalavägen (E4); free/cheap parking at Haga Norra and Haga Södra entrances
places.notes: Source: Visit Stockholm / Lonely Planet Hagaparken listings
places.famousFor: Gustav III's vast English landscape park on Brunnsviken bay — the blue-and-gold Copper Tents, Haga Palace lawns, pavilions and lakeside picnic meadows
places.goodToKnow: FREE; café in the Copper Tents; rent nothing, just bring a blanket — classic Stockholm family Sunday spot; butterfly house is inside the park · Kids: All ages; stroller-friendly

places.name: Under the Bridges of Stockholm boat tour
places.near: Departs Strömkajen (Gate C, Strömma kiosk) by the Grand Hôtel opposite the Royal Palace
places.notes: Source: stromma.com Under the Bridges tour page
places.famousFor: The classic 2h15 city cruise: under 12 bridges, through the Slussen lock between lake and sea, circling the islands with live commentary
places.goodToKnow: ~375 SEK adult; English guiding + multi-language audio via your phone; onboard fika café; best one-shot orientation of the whole city for day 1 · Kids: All ages; ~2h, long for 5yo

places.name: Tom Tits Experiment
places.near: ~40-min DRIVE via E4/E20 to central Södertälje; on-site parking; combine with Taxinge cake castle (25 min further)
places.notes: Source: tomtit.se + Trip.com Tom Tits listings
places.famousFor: Four floors + outdoor science park with 600+ hands-on experiments — mirror maze, illusions, giant soap bubbles; Sweden's beloved kids' science centre
places.goodToKnow: From ~210 SEK pp, under-2 free; open ~10:00-17:00; outdoor experiment park open through 27 Sep 2026 — August is peak season; suits ages 2-17, plan 3-4h · Kids: All ages (hands-on)

places.name: Stockholm Quality Outlet
places.near: Flyginfarten 4, Barkarby, Järfälla, about 20 minutes from central Stockholm near the E18
places.order: Discounted Scandinavian and international fashion, sports and homeware (Hugo Boss, GANT, Filippa K, Nudie Jeans, Peak Performance, Nike, Le Creuset)
places.famousFor: Sweden's first and largest premium-brand outlet, in Barkarby (Järfälla); around 70 shops carrying 200+ brands at 30-70% off
places.goodToKnow: Up to 4 hours free parking; two children's playgrounds plus restaurants and cafés on site · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Åhléns City
places.near: On Klarabergsgatan at Sergels torg, above T-Centralen (metro/commuter hub)
places.order: Scandinavian fashion and beauty, homeware, cosmetics and gifts across multiple floors
places.famousFor: Sweden's largest department store, flagship on Sergels torg in central Stockholm
places.goodToKnow: Multi-floor flagship with food hall; central, busy on weekends · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Drottninggatan
places.order: Shop high-street stores, browse bookshops and cafés, walk down toward Gamla Stan
places.famousFor: Drottninggatan ('Queen Street') — Stockholm's main pedestrian shopping street running from the Old Town area up through the city centre
places.goodToKnow: Largely pedestrianized; connects Norrmalm to the Old Town · Kids: All ages

places.name: Steninge Slott & Slottsby
places.area: Märsta/Sigtuna (10 min from Sigtuna old town)
places.near: Short DRIVE off the Sigtuna-Märsta road; free countryside parking; do it on the same day as Sigtuna
places.notes: Source: Yelp/Tripadvisor Steninge Slottsby listings + steningeslottsby info
places.famousFor: 1690s baroque lakeside palace by Tessin the Younger whose great stone barn is now a beloved café, interiors/craft shopping village and gallery
places.goodToKnow: Stenladan barn open Mon-Fri 11-18, Sat-Sun 10-17; palace park daily ~12:00-16:30; palace privately owned but gardens strollable; hidden-gem fika + shopping stop by Lake Mälaren · Kids: All ages (crafts village)

places.name: Westfield Mall of Scandinavia
places.near: 10-15 min DRIVE north of centre; 3,700 free parking spaces incl. EV chargers — easiest big-shopping day with the van
places.notes: Source: westfield.com + Parkopedia Mall of Scandinavia pages
places.famousFor: The Nordics' flagship mega-mall — ~224 stores and restaurants on two retail levels next to the national arena
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10:00-21:00; dining terrace + cinema for a rainy evening; combine with Stockholm Quality Outlet (already saved) for a full shopping day · Kids: All ages (mall)

places.name: NK — Nordiska Kompaniet
places.near: Hamngatan opposite Kungsträdgården; 5-min walk from T-Centralen; city garages (Gallerian/Sergel) nearby
places.notes: Source: nk.se + Visit Stockholm NK page
places.famousFor: Stockholm's legendary 1915 luxury department store — Swedish and international designer brands under the rotating NK clock
places.goodToKnow: Mon-Fri 10-20, Sat 10-18, Sun 11-17; Swedish design floor + food hall great for gifts; tax-free shopping desk for non-EU visitors · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Västerlånggatan — Gamla Stan shopping lane
places.near: Runs the length of Gamla Stan from Mynttorget to Järntorget; metro Gamla Stan; park at Slussen and walk in
places.notes: Source: Visit Stockholm Gamla Stan guides
places.famousFor: Medieval old town's main artery packed with Swedish handicraft, Dala horses, Viking souvenirs, chocolatiers and design boutiques
places.goodToKnow: Duck into side alleys for quieter artisan shops; Science Fiction Bokhandeln (fantasy/comics store) on Västerlånggatan 48 is a cult stop for the teens; gets crowded midday · Kids: All ages; crowded

places.name: Monteliusvägen
places.near: Södermalm heights; 600 m walk from Mariatorget metro or 950 m from Slussen; pedestrian-only path
places.famousFor: 400 m cliffside boardwalk with THE postcard panorama of City Hall, Riddarholmen spires and Gamla Stan across the water — best at sunset
places.goodToKnow: FREE, open always; golden hour (~20:30 in mid-Aug) gives the iconic shot; charming wooden houses and Ivar Los park along the path; benches for a picnic with a view · Kids: All ages; narrow path

places.name: Skinnarviksberget
places.near: Short climb from Zinkensdamm metro on Södermalm; 10-min walk west of Monteliusvägen — do both in one photo walk
places.famousFor: Stockholm's highest natural point — bare rock summit where locals gather with picnics for sweeping sunset views over Riddarfjärden and City Hall
places.goodToKnow: FREE; rougher and more local than Monteliusvägen — proper rock scramble the teens will enjoy; bring a blanket and snacks like the Stockholmers do · Kids: Rocky scramble; hold 5yo

places.name: Katarinahissen viewpoint (Slussen)
places.near: At Slussen metro; ride the lift (reopened 2023) or walk the footbridge from Mosebacke torg if the lift is closed
places.famousFor: Historic 1930s elevator and footbridge 11 storeys above Slussen — free platform looking over Gamla Stan, the harbour and Gröna Lund
places.goodToKnow: Viewing platform FREE; Gondolen cocktail bar/restaurant hangs under the walkway; quick 15-min stop en route between Gamla Stan and Södermalm · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (lift up)

places.name: Stortorget & Mårten Trotzigs Gränd
places.near: Heart of Gamla Stan by the Nobel Prize Museum; guard-change, palace and shopping all within 3 minutes' walk
places.notes: Source: Visit Stockholm / danny-cph.com Stockholm photo-spot guides
places.famousFor: The iconic candy-coloured 17th-century gabled houses on Stockholm's oldest square, plus the city's narrowest alley (90 cm wide)
places.goodToKnow: FREE; shoot the red-and-yellow houses early morning before crowds; Mårten Trotzigs Gränd (36 steps, lantern-lit) is 2 min south off Västerlånggatan — the classic squeeze selfie · Kids: All ages

places.name: Stockholm Central Mosque (Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan Mosque)
places.near: Metro green line to Medborgarplatsen, 2-min walk; the mosque sits beside Björns trädgård park. ~15 min from Norrmalm (T-Centralen → Medborgarplatsen)
places.famousFor: Stockholm's grand mosque, inaugurated 2000 and named after Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan (a main financier) — capacity 2,000, with library, bookshop, café and restaurant on site; the natural Jumu'ah choice for UAE visitors
places.goodToKnow: Office hours daily 09:00-17:00 (tel +46 8 509 109 00); come early for Friday prayer — it fills up. Café/restaurant handy for a post-prayer stop with kids

places.name: Fittja Mosque (Ulu Camii)
places.near: Metro red line 19 towards Norsborg to Fittja station, then short walk; ~25-30 min from T-Centralen
places.notes: Address/history verified via Wikipedia + fittjamoske.se, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Purpose-built mosque with a 32.5 m minaret by Alby lake — the first mosque in Sweden permitted to sound the adhan publicly (2013); Turkish-run congregation
places.goodToKnow: Worth combining with a visit if you're south of the city; regular five daily prayers held

places.name: Skärholmens Moské (Islamic Association of Skärholmen)
places.near: Metro red line 13 towards Norsborg to Skärholmen station, short walk from Skärholmen Centrum; ~25 min from T-Centralen
places.notes: Address/hours from HalalTrip mosque directory, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Large, active congregation mosque in south-west Stockholm listed as open around the clock — a reliable backup for prayer times when out shopping at Skärholmen Centrum
places.goodToKnow: Listed open 24 hours on mosque directories; the surrounding area also has several halal shops and restaurants

places.name: Hemköp Åhléns City
places.near: 2 min from T-Centralen / Sergels torg, directly on Klarabergsgatan
places.famousFor: Big 2,000 m² full-range supermarket in the basement of Åhléns City department store — the most convenient proper grocery run from a Norrmalm hotel, open 07:00-23:00 every day
places.goodToKnow: Hot-food buffet, bakery, fruit, kids' snacks, self-checkout (card only lanes); fish and deli counters. Look for 'halal' labelling on some chicken lines but don't assume — most meat is not halal

places.name: ICA Nära Sveavägen
places.near: On Sveavägen near Rådmansgatan/Odenplan metro stations (green line), walkable from most Vasastan apartments
places.notes: Location via ICA store listings search, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Handy neighbourhood ICA on Sveavägen for the Vasastan end of the stay — milk, bread, fruit, nappies and everyday basics without going into the city crush
places.goodToKnow: ICA and Coop stores dot every few blocks in Norrmalm/Vasastan — whichever is nearest your apartment works; almost all are open until 21:00-23:00 daily

places.name: Himalaya Livs (halal grocery & butcher)
places.near: Bus 4 or metro red line to Stadion/Tekniska Högskolan, on Valhallavägen; ~10-15 min from Norrmalm
places.famousFor: One of Stockholm's oldest halal/South-Asian grocers — halal-certified fresh meat counter plus rice, spices, lentils, dates and Middle-Eastern/Asian pantry staples, unusually central location
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon-Fri 09:00-21:00, Sat-Sun 10:00-20:00 (tel 08-673 13 52). For a bigger halal-meat selection, JD Saluhall in Rinkeby has several halal butchers under one roof

places.name: Pressbyrån & 7-Eleven (late-night convenience)
places.near: Multiple branches around Central Station, Sergels torg, Odenplan; several open very late or 24h at the station
places.notes: General chain info; branches ubiquitous in Norrmalm
places.famousFor: Sweden's two convenience chains, on nearly every corner around T-Centralen and Central Station — the late-night/early-morning fallback for water, snacks, coffee and SL travel tickets
places.goodToKnow: Also the easiest place to buy prepaid SIM top-ups (Comviq/Telia) and SL public-transport cards; card payment always accepted · Location needs verification
places.address: Central Station and Sergels torg area, Norrmalm

places.name: Apoteket C W Scheele (24h pharmacy)
places.near: On Klarabergsgatan directly opposite Åhléns City, 2 min from T-Centralen
places.notes: 24h status re-verified via apoteket.se listings, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Sweden's only round-the-clock pharmacy — open 24/7 all year, with prescription service and full OTC range; the address every visiting family should save
places.goodToKnow: Confirmed still 24h as of 2026 (tel 0771-45 04 50). Night visits use a service window; staff speak English. Children's paracetamol (Alvedon) etc. available OTC

places.name: Apotek Hjärtat Åhléns City
places.near: Inside Åhléns City at Sergels torg, 2 min from T-Centralen
places.famousFor: Big daytime pharmacy inside Åhléns City department store — same building as the Hemköp supermarket, so one stop covers groceries and pharmacy
places.goodToKnow: There is also a LloydsApotek inside Central Station (Centralplan 15, lower level, Mon-Fri 06:00-21:00, Sat-Sun 09:00-20:00) if you're passing through

places.name: 1177 — Sweden's 24h healthcare advice line
places.near: Dial 1177 from a Swedish SIM; from a foreign (UAE) mobile call +46 771 11 77 00. Life-threatening emergency: 112
places.famousFor: Free nurse-on-call service, 24/7, with advice in English — tells you whether to self-treat, visit a närakut (urgent care) or go to the ER; call this FIRST for anything non-life-threatening
places.goodToKnow: No charge beyond the call itself; nurses can direct you to the nearest open clinic for children. Save both numbers before travel · Location needs verification

places.name: Astrid Lindgren Children's Hospital — Pediatric ER (Barnakuten)
places.near: Own entrance at Anna Steckséns gata 35 (around the corner from the main Eugeniavägen 23 entrance). Taxi ~10-15 min from Norrmalm, or metro to Odenplan then bus 507/61 towards Karolinska
places.notes: Barnakut entrance/address verified via karolinska.se, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Stockholm's main children's emergency department (24/7, year-round) at Karolinska University Hospital — the place for serious illness or injury in kids; sickest children triaged first
places.goodToKnow: For adults, the nearest major ER is Capio S:t Görans Hospital (Sankt Göransplan 1, Kungsholmen). Call 1177 first unless it's urgent; bring passports and travel-insurance details · Location needs verification
places.address: Anna Steckséns gata 35, 171 64 Solna (Karolinska University Hospital)

places.name: Tvättomaten (self-service laundromat)
places.near: Metro green line to Odenplan, 3-min walk up Västmannagatan
places.notes: Hours/address verified via Yelp + Visit Stockholm, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: Practically the only true self-service laundromat in central Stockholm — wash it yourself or drop off for service washing, right by Odenplan opposite Gustav Vasa church
places.goodToKnow: Mon-Fri 08:30-18:30, Sat 09:30-13:00, CLOSED Sunday — plan mid-trip wash for a weekday. Drop-off service is easiest with 7 people's laundry

places.name: Kjell & Company — Gallerian (electronics & adapters)
places.near: Inside Gallerian mall (Hamngatan 26 entrance), 4 min from T-Centralen/Kungsträdgården
places.notes: Store/address verified via kjell.com + gallerian.se, Jul 2026
places.famousFor: The go-to Swedish chain for chargers, UK/EU plug adapters, cables, power banks and phone accessories — helpful English-speaking staff; Gallerian mall also houses Elgiganten and a Tele2 shop for SIMs
places.goodToKnow: There's also a Kjell & Company branch inside Stockholm Central Station if that's closer. Sweden uses EU two-pin plugs (type C/F), 230V — UAE devices need an adapter only · Location needs verification

places.name: FOREX Bank — Central Station (currency exchange)
places.near: Inside Stockholm Central Station, Centralplan 15; T-Centralen metro connects underneath
places.famousFor: Main currency-exchange desk at Stockholm Central Station — AED/USD/EUR to SEK, Western Union transfers; long opening hours 7 days a week
places.goodToKnow: Mon-Fri 06:00-21:00, Sat-Sun 09:00-20:00 (tel +46 10 211 16 04). IMPORTANT: Sweden is close to cashless — cards/Apple Pay work everywhere including toilets and buses (buses don't take cash at all). Exchange only a small amount, if any

places.name: SL Public Transport — tickets & SL app
places.near: T-Centralen is the hub, 5 min from most Norrmalm hotels; SL Center at Sergels torg/Central Station for help
places.notes: SL standard fare rules; check sl.se for Aug 2026 prices
places.famousFor: Stockholm's metro/bus/ferry network — tap a contactless bank card or phone directly at the gates (each traveller taps own card); kids under 7 travel free with a paying adult, and up to 6 kids under 12 ride free with an adult on weekends
places.goodToKnow: A 75-min single covers metro+bus+tram+Djurgården ferry; 72-hour passes via the SL app often pay off for a 5-day family stay. No cash accepted on board buses · Location needs verification
